title: Reach >= 1,000 reviewed records (>= 2 frameworks, >= 2 subjects)
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. The M3 scale target, sustaining precision and provenance while reaching a thousand reviewed records.
objective: Reach >= 1,000 cumulative educator-reviewed records across >= 2 frameworks and >= 2 subjects, delivered to a consumer.
acceptanceCriteria: >= 1,000 records cumulatively across >= 2 frameworks and >= 2 subjects, all educator-accepted with evidence; gate + provenance complete.
Precision audit re-run and >= 95%; regressions halt scaling until fixed.
All identifiers resolve; exports validate; coverage ledger updated; delivered to a consumer.
